title: Victoria Creighton
content:
Deputy General Counsel
People.ContactEmail vcreighton@mccarthy.ca
- Osgoode Hall Law School
As Deputy General Counsel, Victoria works with our clients and lawyers managing risk, conflicts of interest, client and vendor contractual arrangements, as well as policy and protocol development. Victoria is a member of the Firm’s Leadership Team.
Victoria is a board member of the Osgoode Hall Law School Alumni Board and she served eight years as a board member of the Community and Legal Aid Services Program at Osgoode Hall Law School.  She was the past Chair of the Young Advocates’ Standing Committee and sat as an ex-officio member of the Board of Directors of The Advocates’ Society.
Prior to joining McCarthy Tétrault, Victoria was Associate General Counsel at a major national law firm and before that, practiced as a corporate and tax litigator. She also worked in a multinational consulting firm’s strategy group, advising clients in global resource industries and taught both ethics and professional responsibility in the Faculty of Engineering  at Queen’s University.
